perturbation.original_text: Company shall be responsible for all costs relating to the development of the Recipes and Deliverables, except for costs and expenses relating to the involvement of Reed's personnel and contractors.
perturbation.changed_text: Company shall be responsible for 50% of all costs relating to the development of the Recipes and Deliverables, except for costs and expenses relating to the involvement of Reed's personnel and contractors.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the financial responsibility for recipe development. Originally, the Company was responsible for all costs. The modified text now states that the Company is only responsible for 50% of all costs which introduces a conflict in cost allocation. This is conflicting with the other sections where the company is held fully responsible.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In exchange for Company's contributions and obligations under this Agreement, Reed's grants Company the exclusive right to manufacture, package, promote, sell and distribute the Products (if and to the extent approved by the Development Committee), subject to the terms and conditions of a separate Manufacturing and Distribution Agreement to be entered into by the parties concurrently with this Agreement, as it may be amended, modified, supplemented or restated from time to time (the "Manufacturing and Distribution Agreement"). Company shall be responsible for 50% of all costs relating to the development of the Recipes and Deliverables, except for costs and expenses relating to the involvement of Reed's personnel and contractors.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the Company was responsible for all costs. The changed text states they are responsible for 50% of the costs. The contradicted text also restates that the company is responsible for 50% of all costs. Since the original text states the company is responsible for all costs relating to the development of the Recipes and Deliverables, except for costs and expenses relating to the involvement of Reed's personnel and contractors. changing it to 50% creates a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Reed's will exclusively own all Deliverables. Company will and hereby does, without further consideration, irrevocably assign to Reed's any and all worldwide right, title or interest that Company may now or hereafter possess in or to the Deliverables in perpetuity (or the maximum period permitted by Applicable Laws and Regulations) and Reed's accepts such assignment.
perturbation.changed_text: Reed's will exclusively own all Deliverables. Company will and hereby does, without further consideration, irrevocably assign to Reed's any and all worldwide right, title or interest that Company may now or hereafter possess in or to the Deliverables for the duration of this agreement (or the maximum period permitted by Applicable Laws and Regulations) and Reed's accepts such assignment.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement states that Company will irrevocably assign all rights to Deliverables to Reed's 'in perpetuity'. The modified text changes this to 'for the duration of this agreement', creating a contradiction. This means that after the agreement ends, the ownership of Deliverables is now ambiguous, and causes in-text contradiction. It now contradicts the original intent of Reed's owning the deliverables in perpetuity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (ii) Company acknowledges that all rights of ownership of Reed's Intellectual Property, Deliverables and the goodwill symbolized thereby shall belong exclusively to and inure to the benefit of Reed's. Company shall not at any time acquire any rights, title or interest in Reed's Intellectual Property or Deliverables. Company agrees that it will not at any time contest the ownership or validity of any Reed's Intellectual Property or Deliverables, nor register or attempt to register any rights with respect to Reed's Intellectual Property, nor do anything that would jeopardize or diminish Reed's rights to or the value of Reed's Intellectual Property or Deliverables.
perturbation.justification: The original text grants Reed's ownership of deliverables 'in perpetuity', while the changed text limits it 'for the duration of this agreement'. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states that 'all rights of ownership of Reed's Intellectual Property, Deliverables and the goodwill symbolized thereby shall belong exclusively to and inure to the benefit of Reed's'. The changed text introduces ambiguity about ownership after the agreement ends, conflicting with the clear and perpetual ownership established for Reed's in the contradicted text. This falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time if any of the following occur: (i) the other party fails to comply with any requirements or obligations under this Agreement, and such non-compliance is not cured within 30 days following written notice from the other party identifying the non-compliance
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time if any of the following occur: (i) the other party fails to comply with any requirements or obligations under this Agreement, and such non-compliance is not cured within 15 days following written notice from the other party identifying the non-compliance
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates a contradiction by altering the cure period for non-compliance. The original text allows 30 days to cure a breach, while the modified text reduces this to 15 days. This directly affects the termination rights and obligations, introducing uncertainty and potential disputes. This introduces conflict in enforcement, as different sections of the contract would lead to in-text contradiction and provide conflicting deadlines for compliance. In the original agreement there is a 30 days cure, and now there is a 15 day cure, which clause should be enforced?
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time if any of the following occur: (i) the other party fails to comply with any requirements or obligations under this Agreement, and such non-compliance is not cured within 15 days following written notice from the other party identifying the non-compliance; (ii) the other party becomes insolvent, reorganizes or liquidates; (iii) the other party makes any assignment for the benefit of Company's creditors; or (iv) a receiver is appointed for Company's property. Either party may terminate this Agreement upon written notice if the Manufacturing and Distribution Agreement terminates prior to the first anniversary of the Effective Date.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the cure period for non-compliance from 30 days to 15 days. The contradicted text states a cure period of 15 days. This appears, at first glance, not to be a contradiction. However, the explanation states, 'In the original agreement there is a 30 days cure, and now there is a 15 day cure, which clause should be enforced?' The change creates a direct conflict because the original text allows for a 30-day cure period, while the contradicted text (and the changed text) specify a 15-day cure period. This inconsistency introduces ambiguity and creates a contradiction as to which cure period is applicable, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes about termination rights. This is in-text contradiction as per 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines... This creates in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced, leading to disputes over contractual obligations.'
